title: Infer neutralization and pit-wave regimes from field-wide anomalies
summary: Safety Car/VSC periods materially reduce pit-loss cost, and pit decisions also cluster in reactive 'waves' after rivals stop.
rationale: Safety Car/VSC periods materially reduce pit-loss cost, and pit decisions also cluster in reactive 'waves' after rivals stop. Even without an explicit flag column, these regimes should be inferable from synchronized field lap-time spikes and same-lap pit counts.
implementation_hint: Create race-lap features such as field median `LapTime_Delta`, robust z-score of field lap-time inflation, fraction of cars pitting that lap, top-team pit count last 1-2 laps, and each driver’s gap-to-wave percentile; interact these with `LapsRemaining_Est`, `Position`, and compound.
expected_effect: Can capture cheap-stop windows and chain-reaction strategic behavior that current per-row tyre features miss.
risk: Heuristic event inference may confuse rain, traffic, or red-flag-like outliers with true neutralizations.
